questions.question: Which of the following is/are not always necessary to observe a shadow?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is/are not always necessary to observe a shadow?

questions.question: ________ help us to see the objects.
questions.explanation: Light helps us to see the object around us. Eyes act as a camera creating image in our mind. The light falling on the objects around us reflects them to our eyes and as a result we can see the objects around us.

questions.question: Paheli observed the shadow of a tree at 8:00 a.m., 12:00 noon and 3:00 p.m. Which of the following statement is closest to her observation about the shape and size of the shadow?
questions.question_html: Paheli observed the shadow of a tree at 8:00 a.m., 12:00 noon and 3:00 p.m. Which of the following statement is closest to her observation about the shape and size of the shadow?

options.text: The shape of the shadow of the three changes but the size ramains the same.
options.html: The shape of the shadow of the three changes but the size ramains the same.

options.text: Both the size and shape of the shadow of the tree change.
options.html: Both the size and shape of the shadow of the tree change.

options.text: The size of the shadow of the three changes but the shape remains the same.
options.html: The size of the shadow of the three changes but the shape remains the same.

options.text: Neither the shape nor the size of the shadow changes.
options.html: Neither the shape nor the size of the shadow changes.

questions.explanation: Both the size and shape of the shadow of the tree change.

questions.question: To see a shadow we need

options.text: A source of light and transparent object
options.html: A source of light and transparent object

options.text: A source of light and translucent object
options.html: A source of light and translucent object

options.text: A source of light and an opaque object
options.html: A source of light and an opaque object

options.text: Many source of light and opaque object
options.html: Many source of light and opaque object

questions.explanation: A shadow is formed only when a source of light is obstructed by an opaque object.

questions.question: Which of the following will not form a circular shadow?
questions.question_html: Which of the following will not form a circular shadow?
questions.explanation: Shoe box will not form a circular shadow as it is rectangular in shape and hence will form a non-circular shadow.

questions.question: Which of the following can never form a circular shadow?
questions.question_html: Which of the following can never form a circular shadow?

questions.question: Shadow can be seen only on
questions.explanation: The shadow can be seen only on a screen. The ground, walls of a room a building, or other such surfaces act as a screen for the shadows you observe in everyday life.

questions.question: All planets are
questions.explanation: All planets are non-luminous body because they do not emit their own light.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a luminous object?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is not a luminous object?
questions.explanation:
The bodies which have its own source of energy to emit light are called luminous bodies. Example: Stars, burning candle, sun, etc.
The bodies which are not able to emit light but are visible only when they reflect light produced by the luminous objects are called non-luminous bodies. Example: earth, blackboard, moon.

questions.question: An object that emit light is called
questions.question_html: An object that emit light is called
questions.explanation: The bodies which have its own source of energy to emit light are called luminous bodies. Example: Stars, burning candle, sun, etc.
